title: Vectors, Matrices, Transformations, and Data Systems · Assumption Clinic
summary: Represent flows and transformations across a small service system. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a matrix-based flow and transformation model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use matrix dimensions to validate addition, multiplication, and transformations.
Interpret matrices as transformations of vectors and coordinate systems.
Solve invertible systems and distinguish existence from numerical stability.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Recalculate the repaired case
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (5 × 8) − (1 × 2).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (5 × 8) − (1 × 2).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0 of the exact value.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Matrix Applications applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
